---
title: "About"
about:
  template: solana
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/ken-osumi
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:ken@foldarc.com
    - text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-3846-8747
---

[日本語](ja/about.qmd){data-langpref="ja"} · [Writing](index.qmd)

I'm **Ken Osumi** (大角 健), founder of [FoldArc](https://foldarc.com). I work at
the seam between structural biology and machine interpretability: reading the
internal representations of protein language models — ESMC and its
sparse-autoencoder features — and turning them into residue-level, falsifiable
hypotheses about molecular mechanism.

My training is in structural and chromatin biology. I did my PhD at the
University of Tokyo in Hiroshi Kurumizaka's lab, using cryo-EM to study how RNA
polymerase II transcribes through nucleosomes and recognizes damaged
nucleotides; before that, in the Siomi lab, I worked on transposon silencing and
the regulation of SETDB1. I was a JSPS Research Fellow (DC2) and a University of
Tokyo WINGS-LST fellow.

At FoldArc I build AI pipelines for de novo protein binder design, aimed at
epigenetic and chromatin-regulatory targets. This blog is the other half of that
work — thinking in public about what protein language models have actually
learned, one reproduction at a time.

## Selected publications

- **Osumi K**, Kujirai T, Ehara H, et al. *Structural Basis of Damaged Nucleotide
  Recognition by Transcribing RNA Polymerase II in the Nucleosome.* J Mol Biol
  435(13):168130 (2023).
- **Osumi K**, Sato K, Murano K, Siomi H, Siomi MC. *Essential roles of Windei and
  nuclear monoubiquitination of Eggless/SETDB1 in transposon silencing.* EMBO
  Reports 20(12):e48296 (2019).
- Akatsu M, Ehara H, Kujirai T, et al. (incl. **Osumi K**). *Cryo-EM structures of
  RNA polymerase II–nucleosome complexes rewrapping transcribed DNA.* J Biol Chem
  299(12):105477 (2023).
